info.title: Quizmon API
info.description: REST API documentation for the Quizmon quiz platform. Supports quiz creation, match hosting, AI-powered question generation, and real-time gameplay via Socket.IO.

tags.name: Quiz
tags.description: Quiz CRUD — create, list, and retrieve quizzes with their questions

tags.name: Question
tags.description: Question CRUD supporting 5 types: buttons (MCQ), checkboxes (multi-select), reorder (drag), location (map), type-answer (text)

tags.name: Match
tags.description: Match session management — create, update, and delete live quiz matches

tags.name: Participant
tags.description: Match participant management — join, list, and leave a match

tags.name: Answer
tags.description: Answer submission and match result reporting

tags.name: Rating
tags.description: Quiz rating — submit a star rating and text review

tags.name: AI Generation
tags.description: AI-powered question generation jobs — create, track, approve/reject, and finalize generated questions

post.description: Creates a new user account and returns the user object (without password) and a JWT token.

400.description: Bad Request — username already taken or invalid input

post.description: Authenticates a user using Passport Local strategy and returns a JWT token.

get.description: Returns aggregated stats for the authenticated user: total matches played, unique quizzes, rank breakdown, win rate, and recent match history. Supports filtering by `week` or `month` (default).

rankCounts.description: Map of rank → number of times user achieved that rank

post.description: Creates a quiz for the authenticated user. Accepts an optional image file upload (multipart/form-data).

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Cover image for the quiz (uploaded to Cloudinary)

get.summary: Get all quizzes for the current user
get.description: Returns a list of quizzes created by the authenticated user, including creator and category info.

get.summary: Get a single quiz with full details
get.description: Retrieves a quiz by ID, including all questions and their sub-type data (buttons, checkboxes, range, etc.).

get.summary: Get all questions for a quiz
get.description: Returns all questions belonging to the given quiz, each with its type-specific data.

get.summary: Check if current user has rated a quiz
get.description: Returns `{ rated: true/false }` indicating whether the authenticated user has already submitted a rating for this quiz.
Returns all ratings for the quiz, along with the aggregate average score and total count.

post.description: Creates a new quiz category. No authentication required.

get.description: Returns all quiz categories available in the system.
Returns all public quizzes that belong to the specified category.

get.summary: Get a single question by ID
get.description: Retrieves a question and all its type-specific data by question ID.

post.description: Creates an MCQ question with selectable button options. Accepts optional media file uploads.

parameters.name: text
parameters.example: What is the capital of France?

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: JSON array of options: [{text, isCorrect, order}]

put.description: Updates an existing multiple-choice (buttons) question. Accepts optional media file uploads.

post.description: Creates a question where users can select multiple correct answers.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: JSON array of options: [{text, isCorrect, order}]

post.description: Creates a question where users pick a numeric answer along a slider.

parameters.name: text
parameters.example: How old is the Eiffel Tower?

post.description: Creates a question where users must arrange options in the correct order.

parameters.name: text
parameters.example: Order the planets by distance from the Sun

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: JSON array of options with correct order: [{text, order}]

post.description: Creates a question where users must pin a location on a map. The answer is validated by proximity to the correct coordinates.
Creates a question where users must type a text answer that is matched against the correct answer.

parameters.name: text
parameters.example: What is the chemical symbol for gold?

post.description: Creates a new match for a quiz. The authenticated user becomes the host. Optionally set time per question, background image, and music.

get.description: Returns the full match object including quiz questions, host info, participants, and match results.

put.description: Updates match settings such as start/end times, time per question, background, or music.

delete.description: Deletes a match. Only the host can delete their own match.

403.description: Forbidden — user is not the host

post.summary: Join a match as a participant
post.description: Creates a participant record in the match. Works for both authenticated users and guests (userId is optional).

get.summary: List all participants in a match
get.description: Returns all participants in the given match, ordered by join time.
Returns a participant's details including their submitted answers.

put.description: Updates the display name or avatar of a participant.

delete.description: Removes the participant from the match.

post.description: Records a participant's answer to a question during a live match.

answerData.description: The raw answer submitted by the participant

get.description: Returns all participants' answers for a match with aggregate statistics: total score per participant, correct answer count, and average response time.
Returns all answers submitted by a specific participant in a match, including full question details.

post.description: Submits a star rating and optional text review for a quiz. The user must have participated in at least one match of this quiz, and can only rate once.

400.description: Bad Request — user has not played this quiz, or already rated

post.summary: Create an AI question generation job
post.description: Creates a new job to generate quiz questions using AI. Must provide either an `instruction` (text prompt) or a `pdfUrl` (document URL).

instruction.example: Generate 10 questions about the French Revolution

targetQuizId.description: Quiz to add finalized questions to (optional)

400.description: Bad Request — neither instruction nor pdfUrl provided

get.summary: List all AI jobs for the current user
get.description: Returns all AI generation jobs created by the authenticated user, ordered by most recent first.

get.summary: Get a single AI job with generated questions
get.description: Returns all details of a job including its generated questions (with status, feedback, and link to finalized question if approved).

delete.description: Deletes a job. Only the job owner can delete it.

put.description: Updates the processing status of an AI job. Status values: `PENDING`, `PROCESSING`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`.

post.summary: Add AI-generated questions to a job
post.description: Internal endpoint used by the AI service to populate a job with generated questions. Also marks the job status as `COMPLETED`.

questionText.example: What year did the French Revolution begin?

put.summary: Review a generated question (approve / reject / regenerate)
put.description: Updates the status of a generated question. Status values: `PENDING`, `APPROVED`, `REJECTED`, `REGENERATING`. Optionally include feedback for regeneration.

post.summary: Finalize a generated question (add to quiz)
post.description: Converts an APPROVED generated question into a real quiz question and links it to the target quiz. The generated question must have `APPROVED` status before this action.

quizId.description: ID of the quiz to add the question to

201.description: Question finalized and added to quiz

400.description: Question is not in APPROVED status
